title: PowerShell BitSight API HTTP Client Patterns - Auth, Pagination, And Response Handling
applies_to: PowerShell scripts calling BitSight ratings and findings APIs
Building reusable BitSight HTTP client wrapper functions in PowerShell
Paginating and transforming BitSight JSON responses in PowerShell

confirmed_facts.fact: BitSight API uses the API token as the username in HTTP Basic Auth with an empty password. Construct the header in PowerShell: `$encoded = [Convert]::ToBase64String([Text.Encoding]::ASCII.GetBytes("${apiToken}:")); $headers = @{ Authorization = "Basic $encoded" }`. Note the trailing colon with no password after it.
Base URL is `https://api.bitsighttech.com`. All BitSight API calls use HTTPS. No custom headers beyond Authorization are required for standard endpoints.

Current portfolio ratings: `$response = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://api.bitsighttech.com/ratings/v1/companies" -Headers $headers -Method GET`. Returns a PSCustomObject with a `results` array. Each entry contains `guid`, `name`, `rating`, `rating_date`, `industry_percentile`, and `factors` array.

Company details (single company): `Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://api.bitsighttech.com/ratings/v1/companies/$guid" -Headers $headers`. Returns full company object with `rating_details`, `industry` data, `factors` breakdown per security category (Compromised Systems, Diligence, User Behavior, etc.).

Rating history: `Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "https://api.bitsighttech.com/ratings/v1/companies/$guid/ratings?limit=90" -Headers $headers`. The `limit` parameter specifies the number of most-recent days to return. Response contains a `ratings` array where each entry has `x` (date string) and `y` (rating integer) fields.

Pagination: endpoints that return paginated results include a `links` object with a `next` property containing the full URL for the next page. PowerShell pagination loop: `do { $response = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri $url -Headers $headers; $allResults += $response.results; $url = $response.links.next } while ($response.links.next)`.

Rate limit is approximately 15,000 requests per hour. HTTP 429 responses indicate rate limit exceeded. Honor the Retry-After header if present. For portfolio-wide pulls across many companies, implement a per-request delay of 250ms to stay well within limits.

All BitSight responses are JSON. Invoke-RestMethod automatically deserializes JSON to PSCustomObject. Access nested data using dot notation: `$response.results | ForEach-Object { $_.name, $_.rating, ($_.factors | Where-Object { $_.name -eq 'Compromised Systems' }).rating }`.

implementation_notes: Build a reusable `Invoke-BitSightRequest` function that accepts a path suffix (e.g., '/ratings/v1/companies'), constructs the full URI, attaches the auth header, and handles pagination automatically by following links.next.

For portfolio-wide reporting, first call /ratings/v1/companies to get all GUIDs, then call /ratings/v1/companies/{guid} for each company that needs detailed factor breakdown. Batch the detail calls with a short sleep to avoid burst throttling.

Store the API token in Azure Key Vault or an Automation Account encrypted variable, not in the script file. Retrieve at runtime: `$apiToken = Get-AzKeyVaultSecret -VaultName $vault -Name 'BitSightToken' -AsPlainText`.

Flatten the nested factors array for SharePoint list storage: `$company.factors | ForEach-Object { [PSCustomObject]@{ Company=$company.name; Category=$_.name; Rating=$_.rating } }` produces a normalized row per category per company.
caveats: The API token in the Authorization header has an empty password — the trailing colon after the token is required. Omitting it causes 401 Unauthorized.
Company GUIDs are BitSight-internal identifiers and differ from any external ID. They are stable and do not change.
Portfolio contents depend on subscription tier. Not all companies in a portfolio will have full factor breakdowns available.
Rating history `limit` parameter is in days, not number of records. A company with a long history but low rating change frequency will return fewer records than the limit.
